description: Establish the full Kafka event backbone for the onboarding platform. All Avro schemas are centralized in <backend/common/src/main/avro> under the namespace xyz.catuns.onboarding.common.events. Each microservice owns its Kafka configuration — producer factory, consumer factory, listener container factory, topic declarations, and error handling — as a local @Configuration class, following the pattern already established by <backend/user-service/src/main/java/xyz/catuns/onboarding/user/config/KafkaConfig.java>. Topic names are injected via the app.kafka.topics.* property namespace and topic creation uses KafkaTopicProperties for replicas and partitions. The outbox publisher pattern from user-service is replicated in onboarding-service and provisioning-service.
functionality: have a reliable, schema-governed Kafka event backbone connecting all onboarding microservices
benefit: so that provisioning workflows execute fully asynchronously, domain writes and event publication stay transactionally consistent via the outbox pattern, and each service controls its own Kafka configuration independently

stories.summary: Centralize Avro schemas in backend/common with updated namespace
stories.description:
Create all 10 Avro schema definitions inside <backend/common/src/main/avro>. Set the namespace to xyz.catuns.onboarding.common.events across every schema to match the common module package root. Configure the avro-maven-plugin in <backend/common/pom.xml> to generate Java classes from <src/main/avro> into <target/generated-sources/avro>. Add the io.confluent kafka-avro-serializer dependency at compile scope to common so all services receive it transitively. Update <docs/schemas/kafka-event-schema.md> to reflect the new namespace.

Schemas to create: UserRegisteredV1, IdentityCorrelationRequestedV1, IdentityCorrelationCompletedV1, IdentityCorrelationFailedV1, GithubProvisioningRequestedV1, GithubProvisioningCompletedV1, AtlassianProvisioningRequestedV1, AtlassianProvisioningCompletedV1, OnboardingCompletedV1, OnboardingFailedV1.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Avro schemas compile and generate Java classes
acceptanceCriteria.given: all 10 .avsc files are present in backend/common/src/main/avro with namespace xyz.catuns.onboarding.common.events
acceptanceCriteria.when: mvn generate-sources is run from backend/common
acceptanceCriteria.then: Java record classes are generated under xyz/catuns/onboarding/common/events and the module compiles without errors

acceptanceCriteria.given: a generated Avro class such as UserRegisteredV1

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Services resolve Avro types via common dependency
acceptanceCriteria.given: user-service, onboarding-service, and provisioning-service each declare onboarding-common as a dependency
acceptanceCriteria.then: they can import xyz.catuns.onboarding.common.events.UserRegisteredV1 and all other generated types without additional dependencies

tasks.summary: Add avro-maven-plugin and kafka-avro-serializer to common pom.xml
tasks.description: Add the org.apache.avro avro-maven-plugin to <backend/common/pom.xml> with sourceDirectory <src/main/avro> and outputDirectory <target/generated-sources/avro>. Add io.confluent kafka-avro-serializer at compile scope. Pin Confluent version to match the broker version declared in <infra/docker/docker-compose.yml>.

tasks.summary: Write UserRegisteredV1.avsc
tasks.description: Create <backend/common/src/main/avro/UserRegisteredV1.avsc> with namespace xyz.catuns.onboarding.common.events. Fields: eventId, eventType (default UserRegisteredV1), eventVersion (default 1), occurredAt, correlationId, producer, userId, onboardingRequestId, displayName, primaryEmail, githubUserId, githubLogin, roleKeys (array of string, default []).

tasks.summary: Write identity correlation Avro schemas
tasks.description: Create <backend/common/src/main/avro/IdentityCorrelationRequestedV1.avsc>, <IdentityCorrelationCompletedV1.avsc>, and <IdentityCorrelationFailedV1.avsc> with namespace xyz.catuns.onboarding.common.events. Fields per <docs/schemas/kafka-event-schema.md>.

tasks.summary: Write provisioning Avro schemas
tasks.description: Create <backend/common/src/main/avro/GithubProvisioningRequestedV1.avsc>, <GithubProvisioningCompletedV1.avsc>, <AtlassianProvisioningRequestedV1.avsc>, and <AtlassianProvisioningCompletedV1.avsc> with namespace xyz.catuns.onboarding.common.events.

tasks.summary: Write lifecycle Avro schemas
tasks.description: Create <backend/common/src/main/avro/OnboardingCompletedV1.avsc> and <OnboardingFailedV1.avsc> with namespace xyz.catuns.onboarding.common.events.

tasks.summary: Update kafka-event-schema.md namespace
tasks.description: Replace all occurrences of xyz.catuns.onboarding.events with xyz.catuns.onboarding.common.events in <docs/schemas/kafka-event-schema.md>.

stories.summary: Update user-service KafkaConfig for Avro and wire outbox publisher to Avro serialization
stories.description:
The <backend/user-service/src/main/java/xyz/catuns/onboarding/user/config/KafkaConfig.java> class already exists and creates a ProducerFactory, KafkaTemplate, and NewTopic beans. This story updates it to wire the KafkaAvroSerializer into the ProducerFactory config map and moves the edu.identity.correlation.v1 topic declaration to onboarding-service, which is the correct producer owner.

The existing <backend/user-service/src/main/java/xyz/catuns/onboarding/user/outbox/OutboxEventPublisher.java> sends event.getPayload() — a raw JSON string — as the Kafka record value. With KafkaAvroSerializer wired, the value must be a SpecificRecord, not a String. Update the publisher to deserialize the JSON payload into the appropriate Avro generated class before sending.

Topic produced by user-service: edu.user.registered.v1 (already declared as app.kafka.topics.user-registered).

acceptanceCriteria.then: the ProducerFactory config map contains KafkaAvroSerializer as the value serializer and schema.registry.url from spring.kafka.properties

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: OutboxEventPublisher sends an Avro record for UserRegisteredV1
acceptanceCriteria.given: an unpublished outbox_event row with event_type = UserRegisteredV1 and a JSON payload
acceptanceCriteria.then: the JSON payload is deserialized into a UserRegisteredV1 Avro record and sent to the edu.user.registered.v1 topic

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: idCorrelationTopic bean is removed from user-service
acceptanceCriteria.then: there is no NewTopic bean for edu.identity.correlation.v1 — it is declared in onboarding-service

tasks.summary: Update KafkaConfig producerFactory to include KafkaAvroSerializer
tasks.description: In <backend/user-service/src/main/java/xyz/catuns/onboarding/user/config/KafkaConfig.java>, update the producerFactory bean. After calling kafkaProperties.buildProducerProperties(), add ProducerConfig.VALUE_SERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG = KafkaAvroSerializer.class to the config map before constructing DefaultKafkaProducerFactory. Remove the idCorrelationTopic bean — it will be declared in onboarding-service.

tasks.summary: Update OutboxEventPublisher to deserialize JSON payload to Avro record
tasks.description: In <backend/user-service/src/main/java/xyz/catuns/onboarding/user/outbox/OutboxEventPublisher.java>, add a dispatch step before the ProducerRecord is built. Check event.getEventType() and use ObjectMapper to deserialize event.getPayload() into the matching Avro SpecificRecord (e.g. UserRegisteredV1). Pass the Avro record as the ProducerRecord value. Throw IllegalStateException for unrecognized event types.

tasks.summary: Remove idCorrelationTopic from user-service application config and set broker-safe topic properties
tasks.description: Remove the app.kafka.topics.id-correlation property from user-service application configuration and the corresponding @Value field and NewTopic bean from <KafkaConfig.java>. In user-service application.yml set app.kafka.partitions: 1 and app.kafka.replicas: 1 — the local broker is a single-node KRaft cluster (KAFKA_NODE_ID: 1 in <infra/docker/docker-compose.yml>) and will reject topic creation if replicas exceed the broker count. The existing userRegisteredTopic bean reads these values from KafkaTopicProperties so no Java change is required.

tasks.summary: Update OutboxEventPublisherTest for Avro record assertion
tasks.description: Update <backend/user-service/src/test/java/xyz/catuns/onboarding/user/outbox/OutboxEventPublisherTest.java> to assert that the published Kafka record value is a UserRegisteredV1 SpecificRecord instance, not a raw String.

stories.summary: Create KafkaConfig in onboarding-service with producer, consumer, and topic declarations
stories.description:
Create <backend/onboarding-service/src/main/java/xyz/catuns/onboarding/service/config/KafkaConfig.java> following the same pattern as user-service. Declare a ProducerFactory with KafkaAvroSerializer, a ConsumerFactory with KafkaAvroDeserializer and specific.avro.reader=true, a ConcurrentKafkaListenerContainerFactory, and NewTopic beans for all topics produced by onboarding-service.

Topics owned by onboarding-service:
- edu.identity.correlation.v1 (app.kafka.topics.id-correlation)
- edu.provisioning.github.v1 (app.kafka.topics.github-provisioning)
- edu.provisioning.atlassian.v1 (app.kafka.topics.atlassian-provisioning)
- edu.onboarding.lifecycle.v1 (app.kafka.topics.onboarding-lifecycle)

Set ack-mode to RECORD on the listener container factory so each offset commits individually after its @Transactional handler method returns, preventing uncommitted DB writes from being masked by a batch offset commit.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: All four onboarding-service topics are created on startup
acceptanceCriteria.when: the Kafka admin client lists topics
acceptanceCriteria.then: edu.identity.correlation.v1, edu.provisioning.github.v1, edu.provisioning.atlassian.v1, and edu.onboarding.lifecycle.v1 are present with partitions and replicas from KafkaTopicProperties
specific.avro.reader is true and value.deserializer is KafkaAvroDeserializer

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Listener container uses RECORD ack mode

tasks.summary: Create KafkaConfig.java in onboarding-service
tasks.description: Create <backend/onboarding-service/src/main/java/xyz/catuns/onboarding/service/config/KafkaConfig.java> as a package-private @Configuration class. Define producerFactory(KafkaProperties) that calls kafkaProperties.buildProducerProperties(), adds KafkaAvroSerializer as value.serializer, and constructs DefaultKafkaProducerFactory. Define kafkaTemplate(ProducerFactory) wrapping it.

tasks.summary: Add ConsumerFactory and ConcurrentKafkaListenerContainerFactory beans
tasks.description: In KafkaConfig, define consumerFactory(KafkaProperties) that calls kafkaProperties.buildConsumerProperties(), adds KafkaAvroDeserializer as value.deserializer and specific.avro.reader=true, and constructs DefaultKafkaConsumerFactory. Define kafkaListenerContainerFactory(ConsumerFactory) constructing ConcurrentKafkaListenerContainerFactory with AckMode.RECORD.

tasks.summary: Declare NewTopic beans for onboarding-service owned topics with per-topic config
tasks.description:
In KafkaConfig, inject @Value fields for app.kafka.topics.id-correlation, app.kafka.topics.github-provisioning, app.kafka.topics.atlassian-provisioning, and app.kafka.topics.onboarding-lifecycle. Create a NewTopic bean for each using TopicBuilder with replicas and partitions from appProperties.getKafka().

Apply per-topic TopicConfig overrides:
- id-correlation, github-provisioning, atlassian-provisioning: standard config, no overrides beyond partitions and replicas.
- onboarding-lifecycle: add TopicConfig.RETENTION_MS_CONFIG = "1209600000" (14 days) — lifecycle outcome events are audit-relevant and should outlive the default 7-day broker retention.

Example for the lifecycle topic:
```
TopicBuilder.name(onboardingLifecycleTopicName)
    .replicas(topicProperties.getReplicas())
    .partitions(topicProperties.getPartitions())
    .config(TopicConfig.RETENTION_MS_CONFIG, "1209600000")
    .build()
```

tasks.summary: Add app.kafka.topics properties to onboarding-service application.yml
tasks.description: Create <backend/onboarding-service/src/main/resources/application.yml>. Set spring.kafka.bootstrap-servers, spring.kafka.properties.schema.registry.url, and spring.kafka.consumer.group-id: onboarding-service. Set app.kafka.topics entries for id-correlation, github-provisioning, atlassian-provisioning, and onboarding-lifecycle using the canonical topic names from <docs/schemas/kafka-event-schema.md>. Set app.kafka.partitions: 1 and app.kafka.replicas: 1 — mandatory for the single-node local broker; these values are read by KafkaTopicProperties and applied to every NewTopic bean.

stories.summary: Implement event consumers and outbox publisher in onboarding-service
stories.description:
Wire all inbound Kafka consumers and the outbox publisher in onboarding-service.

Consumers:
- UserRegisteredV1 from edu.user.registered.v1: in a @Transactional method, resolve the onboarding request by userId and write three outbox_event rows — IdentityCorrelationRequestedV1, GithubProvisioningRequestedV1, AtlassianProvisioningRequestedV1.
- GithubProvisioningCompletedV1 from edu.provisioning.github.v1: update the GitHub step status to COMPLETED or FAILED.
- AtlassianProvisioningCompletedV1 from edu.provisioning.atlassian.v1: update the Atlassian step status.
- IdentityCorrelationCompletedV1 from edu.identity.correlation.v1: persist atlassianIdentityId and matchStrategy.
- IdentityCorrelationFailedV1 from edu.identity.correlation.v1: set the identity step status to FAILED.

After every step update, check whether all steps have reached a terminal state and write OnboardingCompletedV1 or OnboardingFailedV1 to the outbox.

Outbox publisher: replicate the @Scheduled pattern from <backend/user-service/src/main/java/xyz/catuns/onboarding/user/outbox/OutboxEventPublisher.java>. Map each event_type to its target topic using app.kafka.topics.* values and deserialize the JSON payload into the appropriate Avro SpecificRecord before sending.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: UserRegisteredV1 triggers three outbox rows in one transaction
acceptanceCriteria.given: a UserRegisteredV1 Avro record is published to edu.user.registered.v1
acceptanceCriteria.then: IdentityCorrelationRequestedV1, GithubProvisioningRequestedV1, and AtlassianProvisioningRequestedV1 outbox rows are written in a single DB transaction

acceptanceCriteria.given: a GithubProvisioningCompletedV1 record with success = true arrives
acceptanceCriteria.then: the GitHub provisioning onboarding_step transitions to COMPLETED

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: All steps completed triggers OnboardingCompletedV1 outbox entry
acceptanceCriteria.given: all onboarding steps for a request reach COMPLETED
acceptanceCriteria.when: the last step completion handler runs
acceptanceCriteria.then: an OnboardingCompletedV1 outbox row is written in the same transaction

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Outbox publisher routes each event type to its correct topic
acceptanceCriteria.given: unpublished outbox rows of types IdentityCorrelationRequestedV1 and GithubProvisioningRequestedV1
acceptanceCriteria.then: each record is sent to its mapped topic and the rows are marked published = true

tasks.summary: Create UserRegisteredEventHandler @KafkaListener
tasks.description: Create <backend/onboarding-service/src/main/java/xyz/catuns/onboarding/service/kafka/UserRegisteredEventHandler.java> with @KafkaListener(topics = "${app.kafka.topics.user-registered}"). In a @Transactional method, resolve the onboarding request by userId and write three outbox_event rows using JSON payloads for IdentityCorrelationRequestedV1, GithubProvisioningRequestedV1, and AtlassianProvisioningRequestedV1. Set the topic field on each outbox row to the appropriate app.kafka.topics value.

tasks.summary: Create provisioning completion event handlers
tasks.description: Create <backend/onboarding-service/src/main/java/xyz/catuns/onboarding/service/kafka/GithubProvisioningCompletedEventHandler.java> with @KafkaListener(topics = "${app.kafka.topics.github-provisioning}") and a matching handler for AtlassianProvisioningCompletedV1. Each handler calls a shared OnboardingStepService to transition the step state and check terminal conditions.

tasks.summary: Create identity correlation completion and failure handlers
tasks.description: Create <backend/onboarding-service/src/main/java/xyz/catuns/onboarding/service/kafka/IdentityCorrelationEventHandler.java> with @KafkaListener(topics = "${app.kafka.topics.id-correlation}"). Dispatch on event type: IdentityCorrelationCompletedV1 persists atlassianIdentityId and matchStrategy; IdentityCorrelationFailedV1 sets the step to FAILED with reasonCode.

tasks.summary: Implement terminal state check and lifecycle event outbox write
tasks.description: Add a method to OnboardingStepService that checks whether all steps for a request are terminal after any step update. If all COMPLETED, write an OnboardingCompletedV1 JSON payload to outbox_event with topic = app.kafka.topics.onboarding-lifecycle. If any FAILED with retries exhausted, write OnboardingFailedV1.

tasks.summary: Create OutboxEventPublisher in onboarding-service
tasks.description: Create <backend/onboarding-service/src/main/java/xyz/catuns/onboarding/service/outbox/OutboxEventPublisher.java> following the pattern in <backend/user-service/src/main/java/xyz/catuns/onboarding/user/outbox/OutboxEventPublisher.java>. For each unpublished row, deserialize the JSON payload into the matching Avro SpecificRecord and send via KafkaTemplate. Topic is read from event.getTopic() as with user-service.

tasks.summary: Write integration tests for onboarding-service event handlers
tasks.description: Write @SpringBootTest tests using embedded Kafka and TestContainers Postgres. Publish each inbound event type and assert the expected outbox rows and step state transitions result.

stories.summary: Create KafkaConfig in provisioning-service with producer, consumer, and error handler
stories.description:
Create <backend/provisioning-service/src/main/java/xyz/catuns/onboarding/provisioning/config/KafkaConfig.java> following the same per-service pattern. Provisioning-service does not own any topics — it consumes from and publishes completion events back to the topics declared by user-service and onboarding-service — so no NewTopic beans are needed.

Add a CommonErrorHandler bean with ExponentialBackOff tuned for external API latency (initial 2s, multiplier 2.0, max 3 attempts) and a DeadLetterPublishingRecoverer that routes to a .DLT suffixed topic. This error handler is specific to provisioning-service because it calls external provider APIs in Phase 5 and 6 — the retry timing is different from onboarding-service's internal DB operations.

Also add onboarding-common as a dependency to <backend/provisioning-service/pom.xml> so it can import the generated Avro types.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: ProducerFactory and ConsumerFactory are wired with Avro serializers
acceptanceCriteria.then: ProducerFactory uses KafkaAvroSerializer and ConsumerFactory uses KafkaAvroDeserializer with specific.avro.reader=true

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Error handler retries with exponential backoff before routing to DLT
acceptanceCriteria.given: a @KafkaListener method throws a RuntimeException on a provisioning request record
acceptanceCriteria.then: it retries up to 3 times with exponential backoff before publishing the failed record to the .DLT topic

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: provisioning-service can import Avro generated types
acceptanceCriteria.given: onboarding-common is declared as a dependency in provisioning-service pom.xml
acceptanceCriteria.then: it can import GithubProvisioningRequestedV1 and AtlassianProvisioningRequestedV1 without errors

tasks.summary: Add onboarding-common dependency to provisioning-service pom.xml
tasks.description: Add xyz.catuns.onboarding onboarding-common 1.0-SNAPSHOT as a compile dependency in <backend/provisioning-service/pom.xml>.

tasks.summary: Create KafkaConfig.java in provisioning-service
tasks.description: Create <backend/provisioning-service/src/main/java/xyz/catuns/onboarding/provisioning/config/KafkaConfig.java> as a package-private @Configuration class. Define producerFactory(KafkaProperties) adding KafkaAvroSerializer to the properties map. Define kafkaTemplate(ProducerFactory). Define consumerFactory(KafkaProperties) adding KafkaAvroDeserializer and specific.avro.reader=true.

tasks.summary: Add ConcurrentKafkaListenerContainerFactory, CommonErrorHandler, and DLT NewTopic beans
tasks.description:
In KafkaConfig, define a CommonErrorHandler bean: construct DeadLetterPublishingRecoverer with the KafkaTemplate routing to r.topic() + ".DLT". Construct DefaultErrorHandler with ExponentialBackOff(2000L, 2.0) capped at 3 attempts. Define kafkaListenerContainerFactory(ConsumerFactory) with AckMode.RECORD and the CommonErrorHandler wired in.

Declare explicit NewTopic beans for the two DLT topics so they exist before any failed message is routed there. Inject @Value fields for app.kafka.topics.github-provisioning-dlt and app.kafka.topics.atlassian-provisioning-dlt. Create each DLT topic with partitions and replicas from KafkaTopicProperties and apply TopicConfig overrides:
- RETENTION_MS_CONFIG = "-1" (infinite — dead-lettered provisioning failures must not expire until manually resolved)
- CLEANUP_POLICY_CONFIG = TopicConfig.CLEANUP_POLICY_DELETE (prevent compaction so all failure records are preserved)

Example:
```
TopicBuilder.name(githubProvisioningDltTopicName)
    .replicas(topicProperties.getReplicas())
    .partitions(topicProperties.getPartitions())
    .config(TopicConfig.RETENTION_MS_CONFIG, "-1")
    .config(TopicConfig.CLEANUP_POLICY_CONFIG, TopicConfig.CLEANUP_POLICY_DELETE)
    .build()
```

tasks.summary: Add app.kafka.topics and spring.kafka properties to provisioning-service application.yml
tasks.description: Create <backend/provisioning-service/src/main/resources/application.yml>. Set spring.kafka.bootstrap-servers, spring.kafka.properties.schema.registry.url, and spring.kafka.consumer.group-id: provisioning-service. Set app.kafka.topics entries for github-provisioning and atlassian-provisioning (consumed topics — creation is owned by onboarding-service). Add app.kafka.topics.github-provisioning-dlt and app.kafka.topics.atlassian-provisioning-dlt for the DLT topic names (format: edu.provisioning.github.v1.DLT and edu.provisioning.atlassian.v1.DLT). Set app.kafka.partitions: 1 and app.kafka.replicas: 1 — mandatory for the single-node local broker.

stories.summary: Implement event consumers and outbox publisher in provisioning-service
stories.description:
Wire inbound Kafka consumers and the outbox publisher in provisioning-service.

At this phase, provider calls are stubbed — the handler records an audit log entry and writes a completion event to the outbox. The stub prepares the service for real provider integration in Phase 5 and 6.

Consumers:
- GithubProvisioningRequestedV1 from edu.provisioning.github.v1: write a provisioning_audit_log row (provider = GITHUB, status = STUB_COMPLETED) and a GithubProvisioningCompletedV1 outbox row with success = true.
- AtlassianProvisioningRequestedV1 from edu.provisioning.atlassian.v1: write a provisioning_audit_log row (provider = ATLASSIAN) and an AtlassianProvisioningCompletedV1 outbox row.

Outbox publisher: replicate the @Scheduled pattern from user-service. Read the topic from event.getTopic(), deserialize JSON payload to the matching Avro SpecificRecord, and send via KafkaTemplate.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: GithubProvisioningRequestedV1 triggers audit log and completion outbox entry
acceptanceCriteria.given: a GithubProvisioningRequestedV1 record arrives on edu.provisioning.github.v1
acceptanceCriteria.then: a provisioning_audit_log row with provider = GITHUB is written and a GithubProvisioningCompletedV1 outbox row with success = true is created in the same transaction

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: AtlassianProvisioningRequestedV1 triggers audit log and completion outbox entry
acceptanceCriteria.given: an AtlassianProvisioningRequestedV1 record arrives on edu.provisioning.atlassian.v1
acceptanceCriteria.then: a provisioning_audit_log row with provider = ATLASSIAN is written and an AtlassianProvisioningCompletedV1 outbox row with success = true is created

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Outbox publisher sends completion events back to their topics
acceptanceCriteria.given: unpublished GithubProvisioningCompletedV1 and AtlassianProvisioningCompletedV1 outbox rows exist
acceptanceCriteria.then: both records are sent to edu.provisioning.github.v1 and edu.provisioning.atlassian.v1 respectively and marked published = true

tasks.summary: Create GithubProvisioningRequestedEventHandler @KafkaListener
tasks.description: Create <backend/provisioning-service/src/main/java/xyz/catuns/onboarding/provisioning/kafka/GithubProvisioningRequestedEventHandler.java> with @KafkaListener(topics = "${app.kafka.topics.github-provisioning}"). In a @Transactional method, write a provisioning_audit_log row and an outbox_event row with event_type = GithubProvisioningCompletedV1, topic = app.kafka.topics.github-provisioning, and a JSON payload containing success = true and membershipState = ACTIVE.

tasks.summary: Create AtlassianProvisioningRequestedEventHandler @KafkaListener
tasks.description: Create <backend/provisioning-service/src/main/java/xyz/catuns/onboarding/provisioning/kafka/AtlassianProvisioningRequestedEventHandler.java> with @KafkaListener(topics = "${app.kafka.topics.atlassian-provisioning}"). In a @Transactional method, write a provisioning_audit_log row and an outbox_event row with event_type = AtlassianProvisioningCompletedV1 and success = true.

tasks.summary: Create OutboxEventPublisher in provisioning-service
tasks.description: Create <backend/provisioning-service/src/main/java/xyz/catuns/onboarding/provisioning/outbox/OutboxEventPublisher.java> following the pattern in <backend/user-service/src/main/java/xyz/catuns/onboarding/user/outbox/OutboxEventPublisher.java>. Dispatch on event_type: GithubProvisioningCompletedV1 → deserialize to Avro and send; AtlassianProvisioningCompletedV1 → same. Topic is read from event.getTopic().

tasks.summary: Write integration tests for provisioning-service event consumers
tasks.description: Write @SpringBootTest tests using embedded Kafka and TestContainers Postgres. Publish GithubProvisioningRequestedV1 and AtlassianProvisioningRequestedV1 and assert the correct audit log row, outbox row, and that the outbox publisher forwards the completion event to the expected topic.
